data: Validate the health and completeness of your context files.
data.options#Options: Flag	Description	--fix	Auto-fix simple issues (e.g. missing frontmatter, empty sections)
data.what-it-checks#What it checks:
The check command validates:
Required files exist — project.md, architecture.md, conventions.md should be present
Frontmatter is valid — Each file has parseable YAML frontmatter
Content is not empty — Files have more than just the template scaffolding
Decisions are numbered — ADR files follow the NNN-slug.md naming pattern
Module scopes are valid — Module context files reference real directories
data.example-output#Example output:
Checking context health...
  project.md      OK
  architecture.md OK
  conventions.md  WARNING - empty section "Testing"
  decisions/      OK (3 ADRs)
  modules/        OK (2 modules)

data: Export your project context to a format that AI tools can consume.
data.options#Options: Flag	Description	Default	--format <format>	Output format: claude-md, cursorrules, mcp, raw	claude-md	--output <path>	Output file path (defaults to format standard)	—	--files <files>	Comma-separated list of files to get context for	—
data.claude-md-default#claude-md (default):
Exports to CLAUDE.md at your project root. This file is automatically read by Claude Code and other Anthropic-powered tools.
contextd export
# Writes to CLAUDE.md
data.cursorrules#cursorrules:
Exports to .cursorrules at your project root. Cursor reads this file to understand your project conventions.
contextd export --format cursorrules
# Writes to .cursorrules
data.mcp#mcp:
Outputs JSON suitable for MCP (Model Context Protocol) server consumption.
contextd export --format mcp
data.raw#raw:
Prints all context to stdout. Useful for piping into other commands or manual inspection.
contextd export --format raw
data.targeting-specific-files#Targeting speci:
When you pass --files, contextd exports only the context relevant to those files (matching against module scopes and project context):
contextd export --files src/api/routes.ts,src/db/schema.ts

data: Start contextd as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server.
data.options#Options: Flag	Description	Default	--port <port>	Port to serve on	3333
data.what-it-does#What it does:
Running contextd serve starts an MCP server that exposes your project context as tools that AI assistants can call:
get_context — Retrieve all project context
get_file_context — Get context relevant to a specific file
list_decisions — List architecture decision records
get_decision — View a specific ADR

data: contextd stores all project context in a .context/ directory at your project root.
data.directory-structure#Directory structure:
.context/
├── project.md          # High-level project overview
├── architecture.md     # System architecture and design
├── conventions.md      # Coding standards and patterns
├── decisions/          # Architecture decision records (ADRs)
│   ├── 001-why-typescript.md
│   └── 002-api-design.md
└── modules/            # Module-specific context
    ├── api.md
    └── ui.md
data.projectmd#project.md:
A high-level overview of your project. Should cover:
What the project does
Tech stack (language, framework, database)
Key goals and non-goals
Things to avoid
data.architecturemd#architecture.md:
Documents the system architecture:
System overview and component diagram description
Directory structure explanation
Data flow patterns
External services and integrations
data.conventionsmd#conventions.md:
Coding standards and patterns the team follows:
Naming conventions
File organization
Design patterns used or avoided
Error handling approach
Testing strategy
data.decisions#Decisions:
The decisions/ directory contains Architecture Decision Records (ADRs). Each file follows the naming pattern NNN-slug.md and contains:
Title and date
Status (proposed, accepted, deprecated)
Context describing the issue
The decision made
Consequences
Alternatives considered
data.modules#Modules:
The modules/ directory contains context for specific directories or areas of your codebase. Each file describes:
The purpose of the module
Key patterns used
Gotchas, rules, or conventions specific to that area
Module files should be named after the directory they cover (e.g., api.md for the api/ directory) and include a scope field in the frontmatter.
data.file-format#File format:
All context files use Markdown with YAML frontmatter:
---
title: Project Overview
updated: 2026-05-03
---
# Content here
Decision files include additional frontmatter fields:
---
title: "Why we use tRPC instead of REST"
date: 2026-05-03
status: accepted
tags: ["api", "architecture"]
---
Module context files include a scope field:
---
title: API Module
scope: api
updated: 2026-05-03
---

data: contextd can export your project context into formats that AI tools understand natively.
data.claudemd#CLAUDE.md:
contextd export --format claude-md
Exports to CLAUDE.md in your project root. This file is automatically picked up by Claude Code and included in every conversation.The output combines all context files (project overview, architecture, conventions, modules, and decisions) into a single Markdown document.
data.cursorrules#.cursorrules:
contextd export --format cursorrules
Exports to .cursorrules in your project root. Cursor reads this file to understand your project's context, conventions, and preferences.
data.raw-output#Raw output:
contextd export --format raw
Prints the combined context to stdout. Useful for piping into other tools or copying manually.
data.export-options#Export options: Option	Default	Description	--format <format>	claude-md	Output format: claude-md, cursorrules, mcp, raw	--output <path>	(format standard)	Custom output file path	--files <files>	(all)	Comma-separated list of specific files to include
data.examples#Examples:
Export to a custom path:
contextd export --format claude-md --output ./docs/context.md
Export context relevant to specific files:
contextd export --format raw --files src/api/routes.ts,src/api/middleware.ts
data.choosing-a-format#Choosing a format:
CLAUDE.md — Use if you work with Claude Code. It reads this file automatically on every request.
.cursorrules — Use if you work with Cursor. It reads this file to tailor its behavior to your project.
Raw — Use for debugging, custom integrations, or when you want to paste context manually.

data: The context layer for AI-assisted development.contextd helps you maintain structured, machine-readable project context so AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, Copilot) understand your codebase deeply and consistently.
data.what-it-does#What it does:
Structured context files — Store project overview, architecture, conventions, and module-specific knowledge in a standardized .context/ folder
AI-ready exports — Export your context to CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, or raw text so AI tools can consume it
Architecture decision records — Track why you made key decisions with ADRs
MCP server — Run contextd as an MCP server for real-time context queries
VS Code extension — Manage context directly from your editor

data.installation#Installation:
Install contextd globally via npm:
npm install -g contextd
Or run it directly with npx:
npx contextd init
data.initialize-a-project#Initialize a projec:
Navigate to your project root and run:
contextd init
This creates a .context/ directory with starter templates:
.context/
  project.md       ← Project overview and goals
  architecture.md  ← System architecture and data flow
  conventions.md   ← Coding conventions and patterns
  decisions/       ← Architecture decision records (ADRs)
  modules/         ← Module-specific context files
data.edit-your-context-files#Edit your contex:
Open each .md file and fill in the details about your project. The frontmatter at the top of each file is used for metadata and can be left as-is initially.
---
title: Project Overview
updated: 2026-05-03
---
# Project Overview
## What is this?
A web application for managing...
## Tech Stack
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Next.js
- **Database**: PostgreSQL
data.export-to-ai-tools#Export to AI tools:
Once your context files are populated, export them for your AI tool of choice:
# Export to CLAUDE.md (default)
contextd export
# Export to .cursorrules
contextd export --format cursorrules
# Export raw text to stdout
contextd export --format raw
The exported file is placed at your project root and will be automatically picked up by Claude Code, Cursor, or any other AI assistant that reads these files.
data.check-context-health#Check context healt:
Validate that your context files are complete and well-formed:
contextd check
data.whats-next#What's next:
Learn about each CLI command
Set up the MCP server for real-time context
Install the VS Code extension
Understand the context file format

data: The contextd VS Code extension provides a visual interface for managing your project's context directly from the editor.
data.installation#Installation: Install from the VS Code Marketplace.
data.context-tree-view#Context Tree View: Browse all your context files in a dedicated sidebar view. Click any file to open and edit it directly.
data.health-view#Health View: See the status of your context files at a glance. The health view highlights missing files, outdated content, and structural issues.
data.status-bar-indicator#Status Bar Indicato: A $ icon in the status bar shows whether contextd is initialized in your workspace. Click it to run a health check.
data.commands#Commands: Command	Description	contextd: Initialize	Create a .context/ directory with template files	contextd: Export	Export context to CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, or copy raw output to clipboard	contextd: Check Health	Run a health check and show results in the Health View	contextd: Add Architecture Decision	Create a new ADR with a pre-filled template	contextd: Add Module Context	Create module context for the current file's directory	contextd: Copy Context for File	Copy all context relevant to the active file to clipboard	contextd: Refresh	Refresh the context tree and health views
data.auto-export#Auto-Export: Enable contextd.autoExportOnSave in your settings to automatically regenerate CLAUDE.md or .cursorrules whenever a context file is saved.
data.settings#Settings: Setting	Default	Description	contextd.defaultExportFormat	claude-md	Default format for exports	contextd.autoExportOnSave	false	Auto-regenerate export files on context file save	contextd.showStatusBar	true	Show the status bar indicator
data.file-watching#File Watching: The extension watches .context/**/*.md for changes and automatically refreshes the tree and health views when files are created, modified, or deleted.

data: contextd can run as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, giving AI coding agents programmatic access to your project's context.
data.starting-the-server#Starting the server:
npx contextd serve
The server runs over stdio by default. It requires @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to be installed.
data.options#Options: Option	Default	Description	--port <port>	3333	Port to serve on
data.available-tools#Available tools: Tool	Description	Parameters	get_project_overview	Get a high-level overview of the project	None	get_conventions	Get coding conventions and standards for this project	None	get_relevant_context	Get context relevant to specific files or areas of the codebase	files — list of file paths	list_decisions	List all architecture decisions (ADRs)	None	get_module_context	Get context for a specific module or directory	module — module name or path
data.how-it-works#How it works: When a tool is called, the MCP server reads from your .context/ directory and returns the relevant content as structured text. The get_relevant_context tool automatically matches your requested files against module scopes to find the most relevant context files.

data: Manage architecture decision records (ADRs) in your project.
data.add#add:
Create a new ADR:
contextd decision add "Why we use tRPC instead of REST"
This creates a file in .context/decisions/ with the next sequential number (e.g. 004-why-we-use-trpc-instead-of-rest.md) and pre-filled ADR template:
---
title: "Why we use tRPC instead of REST"
date: 2026-05-03
status: accepted
tags: []
---
# ADR-004: Why we use tRPC instead of REST
## Context
What is the issue that is motivating this decision?
## Decision
What are we doing?
## Consequences
What becomes easier or harder?
## Alternatives Considered
data.list#list:
List all existing ADRs:
contextd decision list
data.view#view:
View a specific ADR by number or slug:
contextd decision view 004
